senators.name: Mitch McConnell
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Louisville (with honors) and J.D. from University of Kentucky College of Law (president of Student Bar Association). Longest-serving Senate party leader in U.S. history. Master of legislative procedure, institutional rules, and strategic political analysis. His deep knowledge of Senate procedure and constitutional precedent represents a form of analytical expertise unmatched in the chamber.
education: J.D., University of Kentucky College of Law
experience: Chief Legislative Assistant to Sen. Marlow Cook

senators.name: Rand Paul
senators.nerdReason: Attended Baylor University. M.D. from Duke University School of Medicine. Board-certified ophthalmologist who practiced for nearly two decades. Founded the Southern Kentucky Lions Eye Clinic providing free surgeries to those in need. Medical training provides rigorous scientific methodology. Applies medical expertise to healthcare and public health policy debates, though sometimes at odds with mainstream medical consensus.
education: M.D., Duke University School of Medicine
experience: Founder, Southern Kentucky Lions Eye Clinic

KY-01.name: James Comer
KY-01.notNerdReason: B.S. in agriculture from Western Kentucky University. Career as farmer, auto dealer, and state legislator. No analytical or evidence-based policy background. Background in agricultural business and practical farming.

KY-02.name: Brett Guthrie
KY-02.nerdReason: B.S. in economics from U.S. Military Academy at West Point and M.A. in public and private management from Yale University. West Point provides rigorous analytical and engineering-adjacent education. Yale master's adds elite policy analysis training. Army Artillery Officer in 101st Airborne. Now Chairman of House Energy and Commerce Committee. Combines military analytical discipline with elite academic policy training.
education: M.A. Public and Private Management, Yale University
B.S. Economics, U.S. Military Academy at West Point
experience: Chairman, House Energy and Commerce Committee

KY-03.name: Morgan McGarvey
KY-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Missouri and J.D. from University of Kentucky College of Law. Career as attorney and Kentucky State Senate Minority Leader. Legal and political background. Solid policy engagement but not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Kentucky College of Law

KY-04.name: Thomas Massie
KY-04.notNerdReason: B.S. in electrical engineering and M.S. in mechanical engineering from MIT. Invented the PHANToM haptic interface at MIT, enabling users to feel virtual objects. Founded SensAble Technologies, raising $32M in venture capital, creating 70 jobs, and obtaining 29 patents. Lives off-grid on a solar-powered cattle farm he built himself. One of the most technically accomplished members of Congress. Brings genuine engineering and invention expertise to technology and energy policy.

KY-05.name: Hal Rogers
KY-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Kentucky and J.D. from University of Kentucky College of Law. Career as attorney and politician. Longest-serving member of Kentucky's delegation (since 1981). Deep appropriations expertise but approach is more earmark-focused than analytically rigorous.
education: J.D., University of Kentucky College of Law

KY-06.name: Andy Barr
KY-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Virginia (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa) and J.D. from University of Kentucky College of Law. Strong academic credentials (UVA with honors). Career as attorney and congressional staffer. Serves on Financial Services Committee. Solid intellectual foundations but legislative approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., University of Kentucky College of Law
B.A., University of Virginia (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa)
